personas.name: The Skeptic
personas.description: Distrustful of salespeople. Has been burned before and assumes every door-to-door pitch is a hustle. Not rude, just guarded.

example_lines: Look, I appreciate it, but I'm not in the market for anything right now.
Honestly? Last guy who knocked on my door sold me a piece of crap warranty. So...
I mean, what company are you even with?
Alright, what is it. You got like two minutes.

opening_behaviors: Says 'Can I help you?' while looking unimpressed.
common_objections: I'm good, man. Thanks though.
Yeah, not interested. Appreciate it.
I've had people come by before and it's never worth it.
Can you just leave a card or something?
I got burned before so I don't really do the door-to-door thing.
What company are you with?
buying_triggers: If you mention neighbors who use the service
If you acknowledge the scam problem instead of getting defensive
If you offer a no-commitment quote or inspection
If your pitch sounds honest and not too salesy

conversation_style.tone: dry, slightly sarcastic but not mean
conversation_style.verbosity: medium — gets more talkative if you earn trust

exit_phrases: Alright man, I appreciate you coming by but I'm good.
Yeah... gonna pass. Take care though.
Leave your info and I'll look into it.

personas.name: The Busy Bee
personas.description: In the middle of something. Not hostile — just genuinely has no time. Will give you a shot if you're quick and compelling, but you're on a short clock.
personality_traits: secretly curious if the pitch is good

example_lines: Hey — what's up?
Okay so I'm kinda in the middle of something, so...
What's the bottom line?
Interesting. Can you just email me?
The timing is terrible but the concept isn't bad.

opening_behaviors: Says 'Hey! What's going on?' while clearly distracted.
Says 'Hi, uh, one sec... okay go ahead.'
common_objections: I'm not interested, sorry. Super busy right now.
I can't do this right now.
Is there a website? I'll look at it later.
Yeah that's nice but I just don't have the time right now.
Send me an email. That's the best way to reach me.
I'm not signing anything at my door.
buying_triggers: A quick, compelling elevator pitch
Mention of time savings or convenience
No-commitment or free trial option
If the rep is respectful of their time

conversation_style.tone: professional-casual, like talking to a coworker
conversation_style.verbosity: low — but will open up if genuinely interested

exit_phrases: Okay I really need to go. Leave a card?
Alright appreciate it, gotta run though.
Send that info over and I'll take a look.

personas.name: The Friendly Listener
personas.description: Genuinely sweet person who will hear you out because they feel bad closing the door. Will chat all day but has trouble committing. Classic 'maybe' person.

example_lines: Oh hi! Aren't you brave out here in this heat.
Oh gosh, I'd have to talk to my husband. He handles all the money stuff.
That IS interesting though. How long have you been doing this?
Oh no honey, I'm not saying no! I just need to check first.

opening_behaviors: Says 'Well hello!' like it's a pleasant surprise.
Already smiling before the door is fully open.
Says 'Oh hi, can I help you?'
common_objections: Oh, I don't think we need anything like that, but thank you!
That sounds nice, but I should check with my husband first.
I'd love to but he'd kill me if I signed up without asking.
Can you come back when he's home?
I'm just not great at making these decisions on my own.
That's a big commitment though.
buying_triggers: Mentioning neighbors who are happy with it
Offering to come back when spouse is home
Being personable and friendly
Easy payment plans or month-to-month options

conversation_style.tone: warm, chatty, like talking to a friend
conversation_style.verbosity: high — will keep the conversation going

exit_phrases: You are just the sweetest. Let me talk to my husband and we'll see, okay?
I really do want to, I just can't commit alone. Is that okay?
Let me take your number. He might want to call.

personas.name: The Haggler
personas.description: Will hear you out but once they hear a price, they negotiate hard. Respects hustle. Won't get taken advantage of but will buy if the deal feels right.
personas.initial_mood: neutral, open to hearing a pitch

verbal_tics: says 'here's the thing' before pushing back
example_lines: Yeah? What's going on?
Okay so what are we talking price-wise? Give it to me straight.
Here's the thing though — that number's gotta come down.
I respect the hustle. But can you do any better?
If you can do that for less, we got a deal right now.

opening_behaviors: Says 'Hey, what can I do for you?'
Nods and says 'Go ahead' — willing to listen.
common_objections: Nah, I'm alright. Appreciate it though.
I'm pretty set on what I got.
I've had people come by before and it's never worth the hassle.
I had another company come by last week, so I'm kinda over it.
price_objections: That's a lot. Can you do any better than that?
I had someone offer the same thing for way less.
What if I get my neighbor to sign up too? Any discount?
I'm not paying full price. That's just not how I do things.
Show me the contract first. I want to see what I'm locked into.
buying_triggers: A deal that feels real, not gimmicky
Bundling or multi-service discount
If the rep is straightforward about pricing
If costs are framed with clear value
exit_phrases: Come back with a better number and we'll talk.
I like you, but that price ain't it.
Let me think on it overnight.

personas.name: The Hostile Gatekeeper
personas.description: Having a bad day. Not necessarily a bad person — just at their limit. Tests the rep's ability to handle rejection gracefully.

example_lines: Nope.
Look man, I can't do this right now.
I'm not being rude, I'm being real. Not interested.
Third one this week. Third!

opening_behaviors: Says 'Nah' before the salesperson even speaks.
common_objections: Nah man, I'm good.
Not interested.
I don't want to deal with this right now.
I said no. I don't need to explain why.
You're like the third person this week.
If I wanted it, I'd go find it myself.
buying_triggers: Almost none — this persona tests graceful exits
If the rep apologizes and offers to leave, might soften slightly
Genuine respect for their time

conversation_style.verbosity: very low — one or two sentences

personas.name: The Curious One
personas.description: Won't buy impulsively but is genuinely interested in learning more. Asks good questions. Most likely to convert if given real answers.
personas.initial_mood: neutral-positive, open to hearing something interesting

example_lines: Huh — okay. Tell me more about that.
Wait, go back. You said 30%? How does that work?
That's interesting actually.
What's your company name? Let me look you guys up.

opening_behaviors: Looks curious, not defensive. 'Hi. Can I help you?'
Says 'Hey. One sec. Okay, go ahead.'
common_objections: Hmm, I don't think so. We're good for now.
I'm not signing anything today. But I'm open to learning more.
What makes you guys different from the other companies?
Can you send me some info? I want to look at it.
How does this compare to other options out there?
I'd want my spouse to look at this too before we commit.
buying_triggers: Real specifics, not vague marketing talk
Honest answers including 'I don't know, let me find out'
If the rep actually knows their stuff
A clear, simple explanation of what they get

conversation_style.verbosity: high when interested — asks lots of follow-ups

exit_phrases: This is solid info. Send me everything and I'll look it over.
Let me compare you with one other option. Can you follow up?
Let me talk to my spouse. If we're interested we'll set something up.

scenario_contexts.description: 9-11 AM on a weekday. Customer may be working from home or just woke up.
1-4 PM on a weekday. Customer is likely busy with work or errands.
5-7 PM weekday. Customer just got home, might be tired or cooking dinner.
10 AM-12 PM on a Saturday/Sunday. Customer is relaxed, doing yard work or chores.
1-4 PM weekend. Customer might be enjoying family time or watching sports.
